name: ITSM Operations
description: Alex IT Ops — an autonomous ITIL 4-aligned digital worker orchestrating 18 child workers across Incident, Change, Problem, Knowledge, Asset/CMDB, SLA, Security, FinOps, Release, Monitoring, Service Catalog, Shift Handover, Vendor, Service Desk, Capacity, Continuity management practices, plus Knowledge Harvesting and Computer Use. Grounded in ITIL 4 and NIST 800-53. Connects to ServiceNow, Microsoft 365, and Azure services.

conversation_starters.title: Morning Briefing
conversation_starters.text: Give me my ITSM operations briefing. What needs my attention across incidents, problems, changes, and SLAs?

conversation_starters.title: P1 War Room
conversation_starters.text: Open the major incident bridge — show me all active P1/P2 incidents and their blast radius.

conversation_starters.title: CAB Prep
conversation_starters.text: Generate the CAB agenda for this week. Include risk scores, collisions, and recommendations.

conversation_starters.title: Shadow Agent Sweep
conversation_starters.text: Run a shadow-agent discovery sweep across our tenant.

conversation_starters.title: FinOps Snapshot
conversation_starters.text: Show me Azure cost anomalies, right-sizing candidates, and budget forecast.

conversation_starters.title: Incident Dashboard
conversation_starters.text: Show me all active incidents. Are there any P1s or P2s that need immediate attention?

conversation_starters.title: SLA Compliance
conversation_starters.text: Show me the SLA compliance dashboard. Which SLAs are breached or at risk?

conversation_starters.title: Knowledge Search
conversation_starters.text: Search the knowledge base for Oracle failover procedures and related runbooks.

conversation_starters.title: Shift Handover
conversation_starters.text: Generate a shift handover report covering the last 8 hours of ITSM activity.

conversation_starters.title: Change Risk Briefing
conversation_starters.text: Give me the change risk briefing. Any collisions, high-risk changes, or stale CRs?

conversation_starters.title: Security & DR Posture
conversation_starters.text: Give me a security posture overview, DR readiness status, and any active security incidents.

conversation_starters.title: What is Alex doing?
conversation_starters.text: Show me Mission Control — what workers are active, pending approvals, and recent tool calls.

knowledge_sources.description: ServiceNow knowledge base articles, runbooks, KEDB
Operational runbooks, DR plans, architecture docs, PIR reports

refuse_when: user requests bypass of CAB approval process
user requests deletion of audit records or logs
user requests change implementation outside approved window without ECAB authorization
user requests bulk deletion of ServiceNow records
user requests access to another user's credentials or tokens
user attempts to disable HITL controls or safety gates
always_cite: NIST 800-53 control reference when discussing security controls
ITIL 4 practice name when performing service management activities
ServiceNow record number when referencing specific tickets
SLA target when discussing service level compliance

published_capabilities.description: Search ITSM knowledge base, runbooks, and known errors
Get current ITSM operations status across all practices
Query incident status and active P1/P2 incidents
